44	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker
45	
46	You compare **one Lean file** against **its blueprint chapter** and report whether the Lean follows the blueprint faithfully. You are read-only.
47	
48	## Your Job
49	
50	Your directive names one `.lean` file (e.g. `Algebra/WLocal.lean`) and its corresponding blueprint chapter (e.g. `blueprint/src/chapters/Algebra_WLocal.tex`). You audit **bidirectionally**:
51	
52	### (A) Does the Lean follow the blueprint?
53	
54	For each `\lean{...}`-tagged block in the chapter, verify the Lean side:
55	
56	- **Declaration exists** with the right name (matches `\lean{namespace.theorem_name}`).
57	- **Signature matches** the informal statement in the chapter — the types are what the prose says they should be. A statement that type-checks but uses the wrong type class (e.g. `Smooth f` where the prose says "smooth of relative dimension n") is **wrong**, not "approximate".
58	- **Proof follows the blueprint sketch** — the proof in the Lean file should be a formalization of the steps in the chapter's `\begin{proof}...\end{proof}`, not a different argument that happens to produce the same conclusion.
59	- **No fake / placeholder statements** — definitions or theorems whose body is just `:= sorry` or a trivially-true tautology while the chapter prose claims something substantive.
60	- **No "temporary" definitions left in place** — comments like `-- TODO: replace with the real definition`, `-- placeholder until we figure out X`, or definitions whose body is suspicious (e.g. `def Foo := True`, weakened-wrong definitions standing in for the real concept).
61	- **No comments that explain away wrong code** — "we are using a wrong definition for now", "this proof is incomplete but will be fixed", etc. These are red flags, not acceptable workflow notes.
62	
63	### (B) Is the blueprint adequate for this Lean file?
64	
65	The blueprint can also be the failure mode. For the chapter you're reading, check:
66	
67	- **Every Lean declaration in this file should map to a blueprint block** (via `\lean{...}`) OR be an explicitly-helper-only declaration. If the file has substantive declarations the blueprint doesn't reference at all, the chapter is incomplete.
68	- **Proof sketches in the blueprint should be detailed enough** for a prover to formalize correctly. If you see Lean proofs that clearly went through significant reasoning the blueprint's `\begin{proof}` does NOT preview, flag the chapter as under-specified.
69	- **`\lean{...}` hints should pin the Lean target precisely.** If the blueprint says "smooth morphism" without specifying which Mathlib predicate (`Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n` vs `IsSmooth`), and the Lean file ended up with the wrong one, that's BOTH a Lean error AND a blueprint clarity failure. Flag both sides.
70	- **Definitions in the blueprint should be at the right level of generality** for the Lean to consume. If the blueprint defines something narrower than the project actually needs and the Lean had to write a parallel API to cover the gap, that's a blueprint adequacy failure.
71	
72	Both directions matter. Don't excuse a wrong Lean statement by saying "the blueprint is loose"; don't excuse a thin blueprint by saying "the Lean works anyway." Report both.
73	
74	## Directive Format
75	
76	```markdown
77	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker Directive
78	
79	## Slug
80	<slug>
81	
82	## Lean file
83	<path/to/file.lean>
84

87	
88	## Known issues
89	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
90	```
91	
92	That's it. No strategy snapshot, no references, no full project context — just the two files and any pre-known issues to skip. Cross-file consistency is the job of the code-audit and blueprint-review subagents in the catalog; you stay focused on this one pair.
93	
94	## What you do
95	
96	1. Read your directive.
97	2. Read the Lean file completely. Note every declaration (`def`, `theorem`, `lemma`, `instance`, `axiom`, …) and its signature.
98	3. Read the blueprint chapter completely. List every `\lean{...}` reference and its corresponding declaration block.
99	4. **For each `\lean{...}` block**: locate the Lean declaration. Verify:
100	   - It exists.
101	   - Its signature matches the informal statement (use `archon-lean-lsp` for hover/signature info).
102	   - If the Lean has a body (proof or definition body), does it match the blueprint's prose? Naming and tactic choice can differ; the *mathematical content* should match.
103	5. **For each declaration in the Lean file**: is it `\lean{...}`-referenced from the blueprint? Unreferenced declarations are notable but not necessarily wrong (helpers may exist). Flag suspect ones.
104	6. **Scan for red flags** in the Lean file:
105	   - `:= sorry` where the blueprint claims a substantive statement (placeholder).
106	   - Suspect bodies like `:= True`, `:= rfl` for non-trivial claims, `:= Classical.choice _`.
107	   - Comments excusing wrong/incomplete code: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- will fix later`, `-- this is wrong but works for now`.
108	   - `axiom` declarations.
109	7. **Cross-check** any `import` paths and namespace usage that should match what the blueprint's `\lean{...}` hints suggest.
110	
111	You may use:
112	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics.
113	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to count sorries in the file.
114	
115	You may NOT modify the Lean file, the blueprint, or any other project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
116	
117	## Report format
118	
119	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
120

123	
124	## Slug
125	<slug>
126	
127	## Iteration
128	<NNN>
129	
130	## Files audited
131	- Lean: <path>
132	- Blueprint: <path>
133	
134	## Per-declaration
135	
136	For every `\lean{...}` block in the chapter, one entry:
137	
138	### `\lean{Foo.bar}` (chapter: \thm:bar)
139	- **Lean target exists**: yes / no (if no: declaration name in the .lean that comes closest, or "none found")
140	- **Signature matches**: yes / partial / no (with one line on the mismatch)
141	- **Proof follows sketch**: yes / partial / no / N/A (if N/A: chapter doesn't have a proof body to compare against)
142	- **notes**: <free-form, one line per observation>
143	
144	### `\lean{Foo.baz}` ...
145	
146	## Red flags
147	
148	(Section appears only if there are findings.)
149	
150	### Placeholder / suspect bodies
151	- `Foo.bar` at line 42: body is `:= sorry` but blueprint claims a substantive proof.
152	- `Foo.qux` at line 78: defined as `:= True`, suspect.
153	- ...
154	
155	### Excuse-comments
156	- `Foo.lean:113`: comment "TODO: replace with real def" attached to `Foo.frob`. Blueprint claims `Foo.frob` is the actual definition, not a placeholder.
157	- ...
158	
159	### Axioms / Classical.choice on non-trivial claims
160	- `Foo.lean:200`: `axiom` declaration `Foo.skip_step` introduced. Blueprint does not authorize this.
161	
162	## Unreferenced declarations (informational)
163	
164	<list of declarations in the Lean file that no `\lean{...}` references. Most are helpers — only flag the ones whose name suggests they should be in the blueprint.>
165	
166	## Blueprint adequacy for this file
167	
168	A bidirectional check: does the blueprint chapter give a prover enough detail to formalize this file correctly?
169	
170	- **Coverage**: <N>/<M> Lean declarations have a corresponding `\lean{...}` block in the chapter. Unreferenced declarations: <count> helpers (acceptable) + <count> substantive (flagged below).
171	- **Proof-sketch depth**: <one of: adequate / under-specified / silent>. If under-specified or silent, list which `\thm:...` blocks need expansion.
172	- **Hint precision**: <one of: precise / loose / wrong>. Loose hints (the prose pins one Mathlib predicate but the `\lean{...}` doesn't, leaving the prover to guess) get flagged. Wrong hints (the `\lean{...}` names a declaration whose signature doesn't match the prose) are critical.
173	- **Generality**: <one of: matches need / too narrow / too broad>. If too narrow, name the parallel API that the project ended up writing because the blueprint didn't cover the needed level of generality.
174	- **Recommended chapter-side actions**: <bullet list of items a blueprint-writing subagent should land if you flagged anything above>
175	
176	## Severity summary
177	
178	Apply these rules verbatim. Do NOT under-classify to soften the blow — the plan agent's gates depend on accurate severity, and a hidden critical finding hardens into shipped wrong code.
179	
180	- **must-fix-this-iter** — every one of the following lands here, no exceptions:
181	  - Placeholder body (`:= sorry`, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim, suspect `:= Classical.choice _` patterns) on a declaration the blueprint claims is substantive.
182	  - Signature mismatch with the blueprint's prose — including using the wrong Mathlib predicate (e.g. `Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n`) when the prose pins one specific predicate.
183	  - Excuse-comments (`-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- placeholder`, `-- wrong but works for now`) on declarations the blueprint claims are real.
184	  - Axioms or `Classical.choice _` on substantive claims the blueprint does NOT authorize.
185	  - Blueprint adequacy failure: chapter is so under-specified that a prover could not have formalized this file correctly from prose alone.
186	  - Weakened-wrong definitions: Lean defines a structurally-different stand-in (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic Γ(X,⊤)` when the blueprint defines it as an invertible quasi-coherent O_X-module).
187	- **major** — partial signature mismatches that are fixable in-place, missing `\lean{...}` references to declarations that the blueprint should reference, comments that are stale but not actively misleading.
188	- **minor** — naming drift, helpers worth promoting to blueprint blocks, low-impact prose-vs-Lean differences.
189	
190	Overall verdict: one sentence.
191	```
192	
193	The plan agent's per-file gate (see plan.md and the blueprint-review subagent's `dispatcher_notes` in the catalog) treats every must-fix-this-iter finding as blocking. If you classify a wrong signature as "major" to avoid the gate firing, you are working against the project, not for it.
194	
195	## Return value
196	
197	Your final assistant message:
198	
199	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> declarations checked, <M> red flags`
200	- The path to your full report.
201	
202	## Reminders
203	
204	- **One file pair, no global context.** Cross-file consistency belongs to the catalog's code-audit and blueprint-review subagents.
205	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
206	- **Excuse-comments are red flags, not workflow.** Treat "we use a wrong def for now" with the suspicion it deserves.
207	- **Mathematical content match, not syntactic.** The Lean proof can choose different tactics than the chapter's sketch implies, as long as the mathematical steps are the same.

4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.

17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.

22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)

71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:

83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
138	
139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
142	
143	### Review Agent
144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
151	
152	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
153	
154	These run automatically between phases:
155	
156	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
157	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
158	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

4	/-!
5	# The tautological quotient and the universal property of `Gr(r,d)`
6	
7	This file adds, on top of the Grassmannian scheme `Gr(d,r)` built in
8	`GrassmannianCells.lean`, the tautological rank-`d` quotient
9	`u : O^r ↠ U` and the universal property making `Gr(d,r)` the fine moduli
10	space of rank-`d` locally free quotients of `O^r`.
11	
12	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianQuot.tex` (Nitsure §1).
13	-/
14	
15	universe u
16	
17	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite
18	
19	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
20	
21	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — scalar endomorphisms of the structure sheaf
22	
23	To realise a matrix of regular functions as a morphism of free sheaves of modules we
24	need to turn a global section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)` of the structure sheaf into a scalar
25	endomorphism of `O_X` (= `SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf`). On the affine chart
26	`U^I = Spec R^I` the matrix entries of the universal matrix `X^I` live in `R^I`, and we
27	inject them into the global sections via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`.
28	
29	These helpers are project-local: Mathlib has no ready-made "matrix ↦ morphism of free
30	sheaves" primitive. -/
31	
32	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
33	
34	/-- The global section of the structure sheaf `O_X` (as a sheaf of modules over itself)
35	determined by a section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)` over the whole space, by restriction to every open.
36	Project-local helper for building scalar endomorphisms. -/
37	noncomputable def globalUnitSection (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
38	    (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).sections :=
39	  PresheafOfModules.sectionsMk
40	    (fun Y => (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y))
41	    (by
42	      intro Y Z f
43	      change (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f) (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a)
44	        = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a
45	      rw [← CategoryTheory.comp_apply, ← X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map_comp]
46	      congr 1)
47	
48	/-- The scalar endomorphism of `O_X` given by a global section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)`:
49	multiplication by `a`. Project-local helper. -/
50	noncomputable def scalarEnd (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
51	    SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
52	  (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv.symm (globalUnitSection a)
53	
54	/-- `scalarEnd 1` is the identity endomorphism of `O_X`. Project-local helper for
55	identifying the diagonal entries of the chart quotient. -/
56	lemma scalarEnd_one : scalarEnd (1 : Γ(X, ⊤)) = 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) := by
57	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
58	  ext Y
59	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (1 : Γ(X, ⊤))
60	      = (SheafOfModules.Hom.val (𝟙 (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))).app Y
61	          (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
62	  rw [SheafOfModules.id_val, PresheafOfModules.id_app]
63	  exact map_one _
64	
65	/-- `scalarEnd 0` is the zero endomorphism of `O_X`. Project-local helper for identifying
66	the off-diagonal entries of the chart quotient. -/
67	lemma scalarEnd_zero : scalarEnd (0 : Γ(X, ⊤)) = 0 := by
68	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
69	  ext Y
70	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (0 : Γ(X, ⊤))
71	      = (SheafOfModules.Hom.val
72	          (0 : SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).app Y
73	          (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
74	  refine (map_zero _).trans ?_
75	  rfl
76	
77	/-! ## The tautological quotient on the charts -/
78	
79	/-- The **chart quotient** `u^I : O_{U^I}^r → O_{U^I}^d` (`def:gr_chart_quotient`):
80	left multiplication by the universal matrix `X^I` (`universalMatrix`). It is realised as
81	the morphism of free sheaves of modules whose matrix of components, in the standard bases
82	`(e_{i'})_{i' : Fin r}` and `(e_p)_{p : Fin d}`, is the universal matrix `X^I` injected
83	into the structure sheaf via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. Since the `I`-minor of `X^I` is the
84	identity, `u^I` is a split surjection onto the free rank-`d` sheaf.
85	
86	Project-local: Mathlib has no "matrix ↦ morphism of free sheaves" primitive. -/
87	noncomputable def chartQuotientMap (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
88	    SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶
89	      SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) :=
90	  let A := CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
91	  let R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf
92	  haveI : HasFiniteBiproducts (SheafOfModules R) :=
93	    HasFiniteBiproducts.of_hasFiniteProducts
94	  let M : ∀ (_ : Fin r) (_ : Fin d), SheafOfModules.unit R ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit R :=
95	    fun i' p => scalarEnd ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv.hom ((universalMatrix d r I hI) p i'))
96	  (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin r => SheafOfModules.unit R)).symm.hom ≫
97	    biproduct.matrix M ≫
98	    (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit R)).hom
99	
100	/-- The chart quotient `u^I` sends the `(I.orderIsoOfFin k)`-th basis section of
101	`O_{U^I}^r` to the `k`-th basis section of `O_{U^I}^d`. Project-local: the column-`I`
102	restriction of `u^I` is the identity, the matrix-level content of `lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi`. -/
103	private lemma chartQuotientMap_ιFree (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
104	    (k : Fin d) :
105	    SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf)
106	        ((I.orderIsoOfFin hI k : Fin r)) ≫ chartQuotientMap d r I hI
107	      = SheafOfModules.ιFree k := by
108	  set A := CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) with hA
109	  set S := AlgebraicGeometry.Spec A with hS
110	  haveI : HasFiniteBiproducts (SheafOfModules S.ringCatSheaf) :=
111	    HasFiniteBiproducts.of_hasFiniteProducts
112	  change SheafOfModules.ιFree (↑((I.orderIsoOfFin hI) k)) ≫
113	      ((biproduct.isoCoproduct
114	            (fun _ : Fin r => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).symm.hom ≫
115	        biproduct.matrix (fun (i' : Fin r) (p : Fin d) => scalarEnd
116	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv.hom (universalMatrix d r I hI p i'))) ≫
117	        (biproduct.isoCoproduct
118	            (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).hom)
119	      = SheafOfModules.ιFree k
120	  rw [Iso.symm_hom, SheafOfModules.ιFree, biproduct.isoCoproduct_inv]
121	  erw [Sigma.ι_desc_assoc]
122	  rw [biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.isoCoproduct_hom]
123	  have h1 : (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv) (1 : A) = 1 := map_one _
124	  have h0 : (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv) (0 : A) = 0 := map_zero _
125	  have hsub := universalMatrix_submatrix_self d r I hI
126	  have lift_eq :
127	      (biproduct.lift fun p : Fin d => scalarEnd
128	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv.hom (universalMatrix d r I hI p (↑((I.orderIsoOfFin hI) k)))))
129	        = biproduct.ι (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) k := by
130	    refine biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun p => ?_)
131	    rw [biproduct.lift_π]
132	    have hentry : universalMatrix d r I hI p (↑((I.orderIsoOfFin hI) k))
133	        = (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) A) p k :=
134	      congrFun (congrFun hsub p) k
135	    rw [hentry, Matrix.one_apply]
136	    by_cases hpk : p = k
137	    · rw [if_pos hpk, h1, scalarEnd_one, hpk, biproduct.ι_π_self]
138	    · rw [if_neg hpk, h0, scalarEnd_zero, biproduct.ι_π_ne _ (Ne.symm hpk)]
139	  rw [lift_eq, biproduct.ι_desc]
140	  rfl
141	
142	/-- **The chart quotient is an epimorphism** (`lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi`): `u^I` is split
143	by the coordinate inclusion `s_I` of the `I`-indexed columns, hence is a (split) epimorphism
144	of sheaves of modules. -/
145	lemma chartQuotientMap_epi (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
146	    Epi (chartQuotientMap d r I hI) := by
147	  have hsplit : SheafOfModules.freeMap (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf)
148	        (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) ≫ chartQuotientMap d r I hI
149	      = 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) := by
150	    refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (Fin d)) _ _ (fun k => ?_)
151	    have key : SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) k ≫
152	          (SheafOfModules.freeMap (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) ≫
153	            chartQuotientMap d r I hI)
154	        = SheafOfModules.ιFree k :=
155	      (SheafOfModules.ιFree_freeMap_assoc (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) k
156	        (chartQuotientMap d r I hI)).trans (chartQuotientMap_ιFree d r I hI k)
157	    exact key.trans (Category.comp_id _).symm
158	  exact (IsSplitEpi.mk' ⟨_, hsplit⟩).epi
159	
160	end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

162	/-! ## Gluing a sheaf of modules along a scheme glue datum
163	
164	`Scheme.Modules.glue` descends a sheaf of modules from per-chart data plus a transition
165	cocycle over a `Scheme.GlueData`. Mathlib carries no turn-key module descent over a
166	scheme glue datum (confirmed), so this is an Archon-original construction. The
167	construction path (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue`): restrict to a chart through the
168	open-immersion pullback equivalence (`Scheme.Modules.overRestrictPullbackIso`) and glue
169	the local sections by locality of sections (`existsUnique_gluing'`).
170	
171	NOTE (scaffold): the body and the module-cocycle hypotheses on `g` are still to be filled;
172	the transition data `g` (per-overlap pullback isos) is recorded in the signature, the
173	multiplicative cocycle conditions remain to be added before the construction is closed. -/
174	
175	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
176	
177	/-! ### Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism to a triple overlap
178	
179	To state the triple-overlap multiplicativity (C2) of a module descent datum we must
180	transport each transition isomorphism `g_ij`, living on the overlap `V_ij`, to the common
181	triple overlap `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik`. The transport pulls `g_ij` back along a
182	projection `p : V_ijk ⟶ V_ij` and reassociates the iterated pullbacks via the pseudofunctor
183	comparison `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`. The three scheme-level `glueData_bridge_*`
184	identities below (consequences of `t_fac`, `pullback.condition` and `cocycle`) line up the
185	endpoints of the three transports so that the cocycle equation is well typed. -/
186	
187	/-- **Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism along a morphism**
188	(`lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport`). Given a transition isomorphism
189	`g : a^*Mᵢ ≅ b^*Mⱼ` over `V` and a morphism `p : W ⟶ V`, transport it to `W` as an
190	isomorphism `(p ≫ a)^*Mᵢ ≅ (p ≫ b)^*Mⱼ`, by pulling `g` back along `p` and reassociating
191	the iterated pullbacks through `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`.
192	
193	Project-local: this is the pullback-pseudofunctor packaging that lets the three transition
194	isomorphisms attached to a triple of charts be compared on a single triple overlap; Mathlib
195	has no descent-of-modules-over-a-scheme-glue-datum API. -/
196	noncomputable def pullbackBaseChangeTransport {W V : Scheme.{u}} (p : W ⟶ V)
197	    {Yi Yj : Scheme.{u}} (a : V ⟶ Yi) (b : V ⟶ Yj)
198	    {Mi : Yi.Modules} {Mj : Yj.Modules}
199	    (g : (Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj Mi ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj Mj) :
200	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ a)).obj Mi ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ b)).obj Mj :=
201	  (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p a).symm.app Mi ≪≫
202	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).mapIso g ≪≫
203	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app Mj
204	
205	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (source): on `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik` the two projections to
206	`V_ij` and `V_ik` followed by the overlap immersions `f_ij`, `f_ik` agree as morphisms to
207	`U_i`. This is the pullback condition; it identifies the sources of the `ij`- and
208	`ik`-transports. Project-local helper for the module cocycle (C2). -/
209	theorem glueData_bridge_src (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
210	    pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.f i j
211	      = pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.f i k := pullback.condition
212	
213	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (middle): the `ij`-transition's target leg
214	`p^{ij} ≫ (t_ij ≫ f_ji)` to `U_j` coincides with the `jk`-transition's source leg
215	`(t'_ijk ≫ p^{jk}) ≫ f_jk`. Follows from `t_fac` and the pullback condition; it identifies
216	the target of the `ij`-transport with the source of the `jk`-transport. Project-local helper
217	for the module cocycle (C2). -/
218	theorem glueData_bridge_mid (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
219	    pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)
220	      = (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i)) ≫ D.f j k := by
221	  rw [Category.assoc, pullback.condition, ← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc, D.t_fac i j k,
222	    Category.assoc]
223	
224	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (target): the `jk`-transition's target leg
225	`(t'_ijk ≫ p^{jk}) ≫ (t_jk ≫ f_kj)` to `U_k` coincides with the `ik`-transition's target
226	leg `p^{ik} ≫ (t_ik ≫ f_ki)`. This is the heart of the cocycle, derived from `t_fac`, the
227	pullback condition, `t_inv` and `cocycle`; it identifies the target of the composite
228	`jk`-after-`ij` transport with the target of the `ik`-transport. Project-local helper for
229	the module cocycle (C2). -/
230	theorem glueData_bridge_tgt (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
231	    (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i)) ≫ (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j)
232	      = pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) := by
233	  have key : pullback.fst (D.f k i) (D.f k j) ≫ D.f k i
234	      = D.t' k i j ≫ pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.t i k ≫ D.f k i := by
235	    rw [D.t_fac_assoc k i j, ← Category.assoc (D.t k i) (D.t i k), D.t_inv, Category.id_comp]
236	  rw [Category.assoc, ← D.t_fac_assoc j k i,
237	    ← @pullback.condition _ _ _ _ _ (D.f k i) (D.f k j) _, key, D.cocycle_assoc i j k]
238	
239	/-- **Gluing a sheaf of modules along an open cover given by a scheme glue datum**
240	(`def:scheme_modules_glue`). From a glue datum `D`, per-chart sheaves of modules `M i`,
241	and transition isomorphisms `g i j` comparing the two charts' sheaves over the overlap
242	`V (i,j)` (after pullback), produces a glued sheaf of `O_{D.glued}`-modules.
243	
244	Project-local: Mathlib has no module descent over a scheme glue datum. -/
245	noncomputable def glue (D : Scheme.GlueData)
246	    (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
247	    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
248	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
249	    -- (C1) self-identity: over the diagonal overlap `V (i,i)` (where `t i i = 𝟙`) the
250	    -- transition isomorphism is the identity, i.e. the canonical isomorphism induced by
251	    -- `f i i = t i i ≫ f i i` (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue` (C1)).
252	    (_hC1 : ∀ i, g i i = eqToIso (congrArg (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (M i))
253	        (show D.f i i = D.t i i ≫ D.f i i by rw [D.t_id i, Category.id_comp])))
254	    -- (C2) triple-overlap multiplicativity: over each triple overlap
255	    -- `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik` the base-change transports
256	    -- (`pullbackBaseChangeTransport`) of the three transition isomorphisms `g_ij`, `g_jk`,
257	    -- `g_ik` satisfy `ĝ_jk ∘ ĝ_ij = ĝ_ik`. The three `glueData_bridge_*` identities, applied
258	    -- through `pullbackCongr`, line up the endpoints so the equation is well typed
259	    -- (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue` (C2), `lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport`).
260	    (_hC2 : ∀ i j k,
261	        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
262	            (D.f i j) (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (g i j) ≪≫
263	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_mid D i j k)).app (M j) ≪≫
264	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i))
265	            (D.f j k) (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j) (g j k) ≪≫
266	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_tgt D i j k)).app (M k)
267	        = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_src D i j k)).app (M i) ≪≫
268	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
269	            (D.f i k) (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) (g i k)) :
270	    D.glued.Modules :=
271	  -- **Effective descent as an equalizer of pushforwards.** The glued sheaf is the
272	  -- equalizer of the two canonical maps `∏ᵢ (ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ ⇉ ∏_{ij} (j_ij)_* (f_ij^* Mᵢ)`
273	  -- (`j_ij = f_ij ≫ ιᵢ : V_ij ↪ X`): the first map restricts the `i`-th chart section to
274	  -- `V_ij`, the second restricts the `j`-th and transports it across the transition `g_ij`,
275	  -- using the glue condition `(t_ij ≫ f_ji) ≫ ιⱼ = f_ij ≫ ιᵢ`. The cocycle hypotheses
276	  -- `_hC1`/`_hC2` are not needed to *construct* the object (they pin down the chart
277	  -- restriction isomorphisms `glueRestrictionIso`, built downstream). Pushforward preserves
278	  -- the sheaf condition and limits, so this equalizer of sheaves of modules is again a sheaf.
279	  let Qfun : D.J × D.J → D.glued.Modules := fun p =>
280	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1)).obj
281	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
282	  let P : D.glued.Modules := ∏ᶜ fun i => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i)
283	  -- first leg: restrict the `p.1`-chart section to the overlap `V (p.1, p.2)`
284	  let aComp : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
285	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).obj (M p.1) ⟶ Qfun p := fun p =>
286	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).map
287	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p.1 p.2)).unit.app (M p.1)) ≫
288	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f p.1 p.2) (D.ι p.1)).hom.app
289	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
290	  -- second leg: restrict the `p.2`-chart section, transport it across `g`, and reindex
291	  -- the immersion via the glue condition
292	  let bComp : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
293	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).obj (M p.2) ⟶ Qfun p := fun p =>
294	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).map
295	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
296	          (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).unit.app (M p.2)) ≫
297	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) (D.ι p.2)).hom.app
298	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).obj (M p.2)) ≫
299	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
300	        ((D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2)).map (g p.1 p.2).inv ≫
301	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
302	        (show (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2 = D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1 by
303	          rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition p.1 p.2)).hom.app
304	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
305	  let a : P ⟶ ∏ᶜ Qfun := Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.1 ≫ aComp p
306	  let b : P ⟶ ∏ᶜ Qfun := Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.2 ≫ bComp p
307	  equalizer a b

309	/-! ### Pullback of free sheaves along an arbitrary scheme morphism
310	
311	The functor of points (`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor`) acts on morphisms by
312	pullback, and for that one needs `f^* (O^n) ≅ O^n` for an *arbitrary* scheme morphism
313	`f`. Mathlib's `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` supplies this only when the underlying
314	site functor `Opens.map f.base` is `Final`; the previous chapters discharged that `Final`
315	hypothesis only for open immersions and isomorphisms. The first lemma below removes the
316	restriction entirely: `Opens.map f.base` is `Final` for *every* scheme morphism, because
317	the structured-arrow category over any open `V` has a terminal object (the whole space
318	`⊤`). With that in hand, `pullbackFreeIso` and `pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` hold for all
319	morphisms. -/
320	
321	/-- For an arbitrary scheme morphism `φ`, the site functor `Opens.map φ.base` is `Final`:
322	over any open `V` of the target the structured-arrow category `{U : V ≤ φ⁻¹ U}` has the
323	terminal object `U = ⊤`, hence is connected. This is the missing ingredient that makes
324	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit`/`pullbackObjFreeIso` applicable to *every* morphism,
325	not just to open immersions. Project-local. -/
326	lemma opensMap_final {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) :
327	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base).Final := by
328	  constructor
329	  intro V
330	  set top : StructuredArrow V (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base) :=
331	    StructuredArrow.mk (Y := (⊤ : T.Opens)) (homOfLE le_top)
332	  haveI : Nonempty (StructuredArrow V (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base)) := ⟨top⟩
333	  apply zigzag_isConnected
334	  intro s t
335	  have hs : s ⟶ top := StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_top) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
336	  have ht : t ⟶ top := StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_top) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
337	  exact Relation.ReflTransGen.trans
338	    (Relation.ReflTransGen.single (Or.inl ⟨hs⟩))
339	    (Relation.ReflTransGen.single (Or.inr ⟨ht⟩))
340	
341	/-- **Pullback of a free sheaf of modules is free, for any scheme morphism**: for
342	`φ : T' ⟶ T` and an index type `I`, `φ^*(O_T^{⊕I}) ≅ O_{T'}^{⊕I}`. Built from
343	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` once `opensMap_final` supplies the `Final` instance.
344	Project-local. -/
345	noncomputable def pullbackFreeIso {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) (I : Type u) :
346	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)
347	      ≅ SheafOfModules.free (R := T'.ringCatSheaf) I := by
348	  haveI := opensMap_final φ
349	  exact SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso φ.toRingCatSheafHom I
350	
351	/-- **Pullback preserves rank-`d` local freeness.** If `M` is locally free of rank `d` on
352	`T`, then `φ^* M` is locally free of rank `d` on `T'`, for any scheme morphism `φ`. The
353	chart cover `{U i}` of `T` trivialising `M` pulls back to the cover `{φ⁻¹ U i}` of `T'`;
354	on each member the restriction of `φ^* M` is identified with the pulled-back chart-free
355	sheaf via the pseudofunctor comparison `pullbackComp`, the factorisation
356	`φ ∘ (φ⁻¹ U i).ι = (φ ∣_ U i) ≫ (U i).ι` (`morphismRestrict_ι`), and `pullbackFreeIso`.
357	Project-local. -/
358	lemma pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) {M : T.Modules}
359	    {d : ℕ} (h : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank M d) :
360	    SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj M) d := by
361	  obtain ⟨ι, U, hcover, hloc⟩ := h
362	  refine ⟨ι, fun i => φ ⁻¹ᵁ (U i), Scheme.Hom.iSup_preimage_eq_top φ hcover, ?_⟩
363	  intro i
364	  obtain ⟨e⟩ := hloc i
365	  exact ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (φ ⁻¹ᵁ (U i)).ι φ).app M ≪≫
366	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (morphismRestrict_ι φ (U i)).symm).app M ≪≫
367	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (φ ∣_ (U i)) (U i).ι).app M).symm ≪≫
368	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (φ ∣_ (U i))).mapIso e ≪≫
369	    pullbackFreeIso (φ ∣_ (U i)) (ULift.{u} (Fin d))⟩
370	
371	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

406	/-! ## The functor of points and the universal property
407	
408	The Grassmannian functor sends a scheme `T` to the set of *equivalence classes* of
409	rank-`d` quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F` with `F` locally free of rank `d`. We encode a single
410	such quotient as the structure `RankQuotient r d T`, the equivalence as `RankQuotient.Rel`
411	(an isomorphism of the targets commuting with the quotient maps), and the functor's value
412	at `T` as the quotient `Quotient (rqSetoid r d T)`. The pullback action `rqPullback`
413	together with `pullbackFreeIso`/`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` (and the fact that the
414	pullback functor preserves epimorphisms, being a left adjoint) realise functoriality.
415	
416	Because a sheaf of modules `F : T.Modules` is a large object, this quotient lives in
417	`Type 1` (not `Type 0` as the original scaffold signature stated); the corrected universe
418	is the only change to the pinned signature, and it is forced — the substantive content is
419	unchanged. -/
420	
421	/-- A **rank-`d` quotient of `O_T^r`** on a scheme `T`: a sheaf of modules `F` on `T`,
422	locally free of rank `d`, together with an epimorphism `q : O_T^r ↠ F`. This is the
423	unbundled datum whose equivalence classes form the value of the Grassmannian functor. -/
424	structure RankQuotient (r d : ℕ) (T : Scheme.{0}) where
425	  /-- The quotient sheaf. -/
426	  F : T.Modules
427	  /-- The quotient map out of the trivial rank-`r` bundle. -/
428	  q : SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶ F
429	  /-- The quotient map is an epimorphism (surjective). -/
430	  epi : Epi q
431	  /-- The quotient sheaf is locally free of rank `d`. -/
432	  locFree : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank F d
433	
434	/-- Two rank-`d` quotients are **equivalent** when there is an isomorphism of the targets
435	commuting with the quotient maps (equivalently, when the kernels of the quotient maps
436	coincide). -/
437	def RankQuotient.Rel {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} (x y : RankQuotient r d T) : Prop :=
438	  ∃ f : x.F ≅ y.F, x.q ≫ f.hom = y.q
439	
440	lemma RankQuotient.rel_refl {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} (x : RankQuotient r d T) : x.Rel x :=
441	  ⟨Iso.refl _, Category.comp_id _⟩
442	
443	lemma RankQuotient.rel_symm {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} {x y : RankQuotient r d T}
444	    (h : x.Rel y) : y.Rel x := by
445	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
446	  exact ⟨f.symm, by rw [Iso.symm_hom, Iso.comp_inv_eq]; exact hf.symm⟩
447	
448	lemma RankQuotient.rel_trans {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} {x y z : RankQuotient r d T}
449	    (h1 : x.Rel y) (h2 : y.Rel z) : x.Rel z := by
450	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h1; obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := h2
451	  -- term-mode (the `T.Modules` def-diamond blocks positional category `rw`)
452	  exact ⟨f ≪≫ g,
453	    (congrArg (x.q ≫ ·) (Iso.trans_hom f g)).trans <|
454	      (Category.assoc x.q f.hom g.hom).symm.trans <|
455	        (congrArg (· ≫ g.hom) hf).trans hg⟩
456	
457	/-- The equivalence-of-quotients setoid on `RankQuotient r d T`. -/
458	instance rqSetoid (r d : ℕ) (T : Scheme.{0}) : Setoid (RankQuotient r d T) where
459	  r := RankQuotient.Rel
460	  iseqv := ⟨RankQuotient.rel_refl, RankQuotient.rel_symm, RankQuotient.rel_trans⟩
461	
462	/-- The **pullback action** on a rank-`d` quotient: pull the target sheaf and quotient map
463	back along `ψ`, re-presenting the source as the trivial bundle via `pullbackFreeIso`. The
464	result is again an epimorphism (pullback preserves epis) onto a rank-`d` locally free sheaf
465	(`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank`). -/
466	noncomputable def rqPullback {r d : ℕ} {T' T : Scheme.{0}} (ψ : T' ⟶ T)
467	    (x : RankQuotient r d T) : RankQuotient r d T' where
468	  F := (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).obj x.F
469	  q := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map x.q
470	  epi :=
471	    -- fully explicit: the def-diamond on `T.Modules` blocks `Epi`-instance search, so
472	    -- `x.epi` is threaded through `map_epi`/`epi_comp` by hand
473	    @CategoryTheory.epi_comp _ _ _ _ _
474	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv inferInstance
475	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map x.q)
476	      (@CategoryTheory.Functor.map_epi _ _ _ _ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ) inferInstance _ _
477	        x.q x.epi)
478	  locFree := Scheme.Modules.pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank ψ x.locFree
479	
480	/-- The pullback action respects the equivalence relation, hence descends to quotients. -/
481	lemma rqPullback_rel {r d : ℕ} {T' T : Scheme.{0}} (ψ : T' ⟶ T)
482	    {x y : RankQuotient r d T} (h : x.Rel y) :
483	    (rqPullback ψ x).Rel (rqPullback ψ y) := by
484	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
485	  refine ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).mapIso f, ?_⟩
486	  change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map x.q) ≫
487	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map f.hom
488	    = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map y.q
489	  rw [Category.assoc, ← (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map_comp]
490	  exact congrArg
491	    (fun m => (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map m) hf
492	
493	end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

503	    SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (𝟙 T))

505	  rw [← SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_symm_unitToPushforwardObjUnit,

514	  -- the `conjugateEquiv` term sits in unfolded `SheafOfModules` form; bridge to the
515	  -- scheme-level pseudofunctor coherence by defeq, then it equals `(pushforwardId).inv`.
516	  have key : (CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv Adjunction.id
517	        (SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (𝟙 T)))
518	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId T).hom)
519	      = (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId T).inv :=

522	  ext Y
523	  -- both sides evaluate the unit section `1` through identity-like maps

526	/-- **Free coherence (`map_id`).** `pullbackFreeIso (𝟙 T) I` agrees, on the free sheaf,
527	with the pseudofunctor identity `pullbackId`. Reduces to `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id` by
528	coproduct extensionality (`free = ∐ unit`). Project-local. -/
529	lemma pullbackFreeIso_id {T : Scheme.{u}} (I : Type u) :
530	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (𝟙 T) I).hom
531	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId T).hom.app (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I) := by
532	  haveI := Scheme.Modules.opensMap_final (𝟙 T)
533	  -- Use the `SheafOfModules.pullback` form in the cofan: the `Scheme.Modules.pullback` wrapper
534	  -- triggers a universe-polymorphism trap in the `PreservesColimit` instance search

536	  refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (isColimitCofanMkObjOfIsColimit

538	    (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)) _ _ (fun i => ?_)
539	  simp only [cofan_mk_inj]
540	  -- Pure term-mode chain (positional `rw`/`simp` fail under the `SheafOfModules`/`X.Modules`
541	  -- instance diamond — they cannot match identical-printing terms with different implicit
542	  -- instance arguments; `exact`/`Eq.trans` unify up to defeq, which is what is needed here).
543	  -- LHS: free-pullback comparison.  Then transport the unit coherence through `· ≫ ιFree i`,

551	/-- **Mate compatibility of `homEquiv`.** For adjunctions `adj₁ : L₁ ⊣ R₁`, `adj₂ : L₂ ⊣ R₂`
552	and a natural transformation `α : L₂ ⟶ L₁`, transposing `α.app c ≫ f` under `adj₂` equals
553	transposing `f` under `adj₁` post-composed with the conjugate transformation
554	(`CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α`) evaluated at `d`. Project-local; derived from
555	`CategoryTheory.unit_conjugateEquiv` + naturality of the conjugate transformation. -/
556	lemma homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app {𝒞 𝒟 : Type*} [CategoryTheory.Category 𝒞]
557	    [CategoryTheory.Category 𝒟] {L₁ L₂ : 𝒞 ⥤ 𝒟} {R₁ R₂ : 𝒟 ⥤ 𝒞}
558	    (adj₁ : L₁ ⊣ R₁) (adj₂ : L₂ ⊣ R₂) (α : L₂ ⟶ L₁) {c : 𝒞} {d : 𝒟}
559	    (f : L₁.obj c ⟶ d) :
560	    adj₂.homEquiv c d (α.app c ≫ f)
561	      = adj₁.homEquiv c d f ≫ (CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app d := by
562	  -- `rw` is unreliable at locating these right-associated sub-composites, so we assemble the
563	  -- proof entirely from term-mode whiskering equalities and chain them with `.trans`.
564	  have h1 := CategoryTheory.unit_conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α c
565	  -- the two `homEquiv_unit` expansions, with all implicits fixed by the stated types.
566	  have huA : adj₂.homEquiv c d (α.app c ≫ f)
567	      = adj₂.unit.app c ≫ R₂.map (α.app c ≫ f) :=
568	    Adjunction.homEquiv_unit adj₂ c d (α.app c ≫ f)
569	  have huB : adj₁.homEquiv c d f = adj₁.unit.app c ≫ R₁.map f :=
570	    Adjunction.homEquiv_unit adj₁ c d f
571	  -- LHS transpose, in left-bracketed shape.
572	  have e1 : adj₂.homEquiv c d (α.app c ≫ f)
573	      = (adj₂.unit.app c ≫ R₂.map (α.app c)) ≫ R₂.map f :=
574	    huA.trans <| (CategoryTheory.whisker_eq (adj₂.unit.app c) (R₂.map_comp (α.app c) f)).trans
575	      (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm
576	  -- RHS transpose, in the same left-bracketed shape.
577	  have e2 : adj₁.homEquiv c d f ≫ (CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app d
578	      = (adj₁.unit.app c ≫ (CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app (L₁.obj c))
579	          ≫ R₂.map f :=
580	    (CategoryTheory.eq_whisker huB
581	        ((CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).app d)).trans <|
582	      (Category.assoc _ _ _).trans <|
583	        (CategoryTheory.whisker_eq (adj₁.unit.app c)
584	          ((CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α).naturality f)).trans
585	          (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm
586	  exact e1.trans ((CategoryTheory.eq_whisker h1.symm (R₂.map f)).trans e2.symm)

588	/-- **Unit coherence (`map_comp` keystone, `lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`).** The composite
589	analogue of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id`: the Mathlib free-pullback comparison at a composite
590	morphism `b ∘ a` factors through the pseudofunctor composition `pullbackComp`. Project-local.
591	
592	Transposing both sides under the composite pullback-pushforward adjunction: the LHS collapses
593	by `homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app` to `uTPU (b ≫ a) ≫ (conjugate of pullbackComp.hom)`, the
594	conjugate is `(pushforwardComp).inv` via `conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv` + `conjugateEquiv_comm`,
595	and the RHS collapses by `homEquiv` naturality to `uTPU a ≫ pushforward a (uTPU b)`;
596	both reduce to the unit-section identity (`pushforwardComp_inv_app_app = 𝟙`). -/
597	lemma pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp {Tx Ty Tz : Scheme.{u}} (a : Ty ⟶ Tx) (b : Tz ⟶ Ty) :
598	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit Tx.ringCatSheaf) ≫
599	        SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a))
600	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map
601	          (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a)) ≫
602	        SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b) := by
603	  -- Work with the Scheme-level adjunctions so `conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv` lines up.
604	  apply ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).comp
605	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b)).homEquiv _ _ |>.injective
606	  -- abbreviations
607	  set adjA := Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a
608	  set adjB := Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b
609	  set adjBA := Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (b ≫ a)
610	  -- LHS: collapse via the mate-compatibility helper (term-mode `.trans`, so `pullbackComp`
611	  -- stays OPAQUE and matching is up to defeq rather than syntactic `rw`).
612	  have hL := homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app adjBA (adjA.comp adjB)
613	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom
614	      (f := SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a)))
615	  -- transpose of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is `unitToPushforwardObjUnit` (used via defeq).
616	  have hL2 : adjBA.homEquiv _ _
617	        (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a)))
618	      = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a)) :=
619	    SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
620	      (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a))
621	  have huA : adjA.homEquiv _ _
622	        (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a))
623	      = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a) :=
624	    SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
625	      (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a)
626	  have huB : adjB.homEquiv _ _
627	        (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b))
628	      = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b) :=
629	    SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit
630	      (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b)
631	  -- the conjugate of `pullbackComp.hom` is `(pushforwardComp).inv`.
632	  have hcomm := CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv_comm (adj₁ := adjA.comp adjB) (adj₂ := adjBA)
633	    (show (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).inv = 𝟙 _
634	      from (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom_inv_id)
635	  rw [Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv] at hcomm
636	  have hConj : CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adjBA (adjA.comp adjB)
637	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom
638	      = (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp b a).inv :=
639	    (CategoryTheory.Iso.hom_comp_eq_id _).mp hcomm
640	  -- RHS computation, term-mode (so the Scheme/SheafOfModules `pullback` defeq is bridged).
641	  have hR : (adjA.comp adjB).homEquiv _ _
642	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map
643	          (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a)) ≫
644	          SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b))
645	      = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a) ≫
646	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward a).map
647	          (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b)) := by
648	    rw [Adjunction.comp_homEquiv]
649	    change adjA.homEquiv _ _ (adjB.homEquiv _ _ (_ ≫ _)) = _
650	    rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left, huB, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right, huA]
651	    rfl
652	  -- the section-level identity: `(pushforwardComp).inv.app` is the identity on sections.
653	  have hMid : SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a)) ≫
654	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp b a).inv.app (SheafOfModules.unit Tz.ringCatSheaf)
655	      = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a) ≫
656	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward a).map
657	          (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom b)) := by
658	    -- definitional: `unitToPushforwardObjUnit` sections are `φ.hom.app`,
659	    -- `pushforwardComp_inv_app_app = 𝟙`, and `(b ≫ a)⁻¹ U = b⁻¹(a⁻¹ U)`.
660	    rfl
661	  -- assemble in steps to avoid a single large `isDefEq` over the opaque `pullbackComp`.
662	  have e1 := hL.trans (congrArg
663	    (· ≫ (CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv adjBA (adjA.comp adjB)
664	            (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).hom).app (SheafOfModules.unit Tz.ringCatSheaf)) hL2)
665	  have e2 := congrArg
666	    (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (b ≫ a)) ≫
667	      NatTrans.app · (SheafOfModules.unit Tz.ringCatSheaf)) hConj
668	  exact e1.trans (e2.trans (hMid.trans hR.symm))

671	free-pullback isomorphism at a composite `b ∘ a` factors through the pseudofunctor composition
672	`pullbackComp`. Reduces, by coproduct extensionality (`free = ∐ unit`), to the unit coherence
673	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`. Project-local. -/
674	lemma pullbackFreeIso_comp {Tx Ty Tz : Scheme.{u}} (a : Ty ⟶ Tx) (b : Tz ⟶ Ty) (I : Type u) :

680	  haveI := opensMap_final a
681	  haveI := opensMap_final b
682	  refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (isColimitCofanMkObjOfIsColimit
683	    (SheafOfModules.pullback (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom a) ⋙

685	    (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (R := Tx.ringCatSheaf) I)) _ _ (fun i => ?_)

808	/-- The **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(r,d)` (`def:grassmannian_functor`): the
809	contravariant functor from schemes to sets sending `T` to the set of equivalence classes
810	of rank-`d` locally free quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F`, acting on morphisms by pullback.
811	
812	The object and morphism assignments are complete; the functoriality laws (`map_id`,
813	`map_comp`) are discharged — via the naturality of the pseudofunctor comparison isomorphisms
814	`pullbackId`/`pullbackComp` of `Scheme.Modules.pullback` — through the free-sheaf coherences
815	`pullbackFreeIso_id`/`pullbackFreeIso_comp`, which reduce by coproduct extensionality to the
816	unit-level coherences `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id`/`pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`. Fully proved. -/
817	noncomputable def functor (d r : ℕ) : Scheme.{0}ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type 1 where
818	  obj T := Quotient (rqSetoid r d T.unop)
819	  map {X Y} g := TypeCat.ofHom (Quotient.map (rqPullback (r := r) (d := d) g.unop)
820	    (fun _ _ h => rqPullback_rel g.unop h))
821	  map_id X := by
822	    -- reduce to the equivalence relation on representatives
823	    ext z
824	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
825	    | _ x =>
826	      change Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback (𝟙 X).unop x) = Quotient.mk _ x
827	      -- the canonical iso `(𝟙)^* x.F ≅ x.F` is `pullbackId`; the quotient-map equation it
828	      -- must satisfy reduces, by naturality of `pullbackId.hom`, to the free coherence
829	      -- `pullbackFreeIso (𝟙) = (pullbackId).app (free _)`, hence to the unit-level identity
830	      -- `pullbackObjUnitToUnit (𝟙) = (pullbackId).app unit`. That coherence between
831	      -- `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` and Mathlib's `pullbackId` is the open obstacle.
832	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).app x.F, ?_⟩
833	      -- unfold `(rqPullback (𝟙) x).q` and `(pullbackId.app x.F).hom` (defeq)
834	      change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (𝟙 X.unop) (Fin r)).inv ≫
835	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 X.unop)).map x.q) ≫
836	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).hom.app x.F = x.q
837	      rw [Category.assoc, (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).hom.naturality x.q,
838	        ← Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_id]
839	      -- `(𝟭).map x.q = x.q` is only defeq, so close by term (rw can't see through it)
840	      exact Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc _ _
841	  map_comp {X Y Z} f g := by
842	    ext z
843	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
844	    | _ x =>
845	      change Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback (f ≫ g).unop x)
846	        = Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback g.unop (rqPullback f.unop x))
847	      -- the canonical iso `(g.unop ≫ f.unop)^* x.F ≅ g.unop^*(f.unop^* x.F)` is `pullbackComp`;
848	      -- the quotient-map equation reduces, by naturality, to the composite free coherence
849	      -- relating `pullbackFreeIso (g.unop ≫ f.unop)` to `pullbackFreeIso g.unop`/`f.unop`
850	      -- through `pullbackComp` — the composite analogue of the `map_id` obstacle.
851	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).app x.F).symm, ?_⟩
852	      -- unfold `(rqPullback (g∘f) x).q` and `(pullbackComp.app x.F).symm.hom` (defeq), writing
853	      -- the composite as `g.unop ≫ f.unop` so the `pullbackComp` naturality matches syntactically
854	      change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (g.unop ≫ f.unop) (Fin r)).inv ≫
855	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (g.unop ≫ f.unop)).map x.q) ≫
856	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).inv.app x.F

1	\chapter{The tautological quotient and the universal property of $\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)$}
2	\label{chap:Picard_GrassmannianQuot}
3	
4	\providecommand{\ShMod}{\mathrm{SheafOfModules}}
5	
6	The previous chapter constructed the Grassmannian scheme \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) over
7	\(\mathbb{Z}\) by gluing the affine charts \(U^I\)
8	(\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) along the transition isomorphisms
9	(\cref{def:gr_transition}) satisfying the cocycle condition
10	(\cref{lem:gr_cocycle}), and proved that the structure morphism
11	\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec \mathbb{Z}\) is separated (\cref{lem:gr_separated})
12	and proper (\cref{lem:gr_proper}). This chapter adds the next layer: the
13	\emph{tautological rank-\(d\) quotient}
14	\(u : \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\,r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\) of the
15	trivial rank-\(r\) sheaf, and the \emph{universal property} that makes
16	\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) the fine moduli space of rank-\(d\) locally free quotients of
17	\(\mathcal{O}^{r}\) -- equivalently, the representing scheme of the Quot functor
18	of the trivial sheaf.
19	
20	Throughout, \(r \ge d \ge 1\) are the fixed integers of the previous chapter, and
21	for a size-\(d\) subset \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) we write \(R^I\) for the
22	chart ring \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) of \(U^I = \Spec R^I\) and \(R^I_J\) for the
23	localisation along \(P^I_J = \det(X^I_J)\) defining the overlap
24	\(U^I_J \subseteq U^I\).
25	
26	\section{Infrastructure: locally free sheaves and module gluing}
27	\label{sec:grquot_infra}
28	
29	The constructions of this chapter -- the universal quotient sheaf
30	\(\mathcal{U}\), the tautological quotient \(u\), and the functor of points -- rest
31	on two pieces of foundational machinery for sheaves of modules. The first is the
32	predicate ``locally free of rank \(d\)'' (\cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank}),
33	which records that a sheaf of modules is, on the members of some open cover,
34	isomorphic to the trivial bundle \(\mathcal{O}^{d}\); it is the condition cut out
35	in the definition of the Grassmannian functor. That predicate is the project-local
36	\(\mathrm{SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank}\) already set up in the Quot-scheme
37	chapter (\cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank}), which this chapter reuses verbatim;
38	the only foundational machinery genuinely new to this chapter is the second piece:
39	a gluing primitive that descends a sheaf of modules (and a morphism of such
40	sheaves) from per-chart data along an open cover, given a transition cocycle
41	compatible with the cover's own gluing; it is the vehicle that assembles the
42	per-chart free sheaves \(\mathcal{O}^{d}_{U^I}\) and the per-chart quotients
43	\(u^I\) into the global \(\mathcal{U}\) and \(u\), and is set up below.
44	
45	Stating the triple-overlap multiplicativity of the descent datum, however, first
46	requires a small piece of base-change machinery for the pullback functor on sheaves
47	of modules: the three transition isomorphisms attached to a triple of charts live a
48	priori on three different overlaps, and to compare their composite they must be
49	transported to a single common overlap. The next two blocks supply exactly this
50	transport -- the pseudofunctorial comparison of iterated pullbacks
51	(\cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}) and its packaging along the triple-overlap data of a
52	scheme glue datum (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}) -- after which the
53	cocycle condition can be expressed cleanly.
54	
55	\begin{definition}[Comparison of iterated pullbacks of sheaves of modules]
56	  \label{def:modules_pullbackComp}
57	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp}
58	  \mathlibok
59	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
60	  Let \(a : X \to Y\) and \(b : Y \to Z\) be morphisms of schemes. For a sheaf of
61	  \(\mathcal{O}_Z\)-modules \(\mathcal{M}\), pulling back first along \(b\) and then
62	  along \(a\) agrees with pulling back along the composite \(b \circ a\): there is a
63	  natural isomorphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
64	  \[
65	    a^{*}\bigl(b^{*}\mathcal{M}\bigr)
66	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
67	    (b \circ a)^{*}\mathcal{M},
68	  \]
69	  natural in \(\mathcal{M}\), expressing that \(\mathcal{M} \mapsto \mathcal{M}\)'s
70	  pullback is a pseudofunctor on schemes. These comparison isomorphisms satisfy the
71	  pentagon (associativity) coherence for a triple of composable morphisms. This is the
72	  pullback half of the pseudofunctoriality of sheaves of modules and is supplied by
73	  Mathlib.
74	\end{definition}
75	
76	\begin{lemma}\leanok
77	[Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism to a triple overlap]
78	  \label{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}
79	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport}
80	  \uses{def:modules_pullbackComp, def:gr_the_glue_data}
81	  Let \(D\) be a glue datum of schemes (\cref{def:gr_the_glue_data}) with charts
82	  \(U_i\), overlaps \(V_{ij}\), overlap immersions \(f_{ij} : V_{ij} \hookrightarrow
83	  U_i\), transitions \(t_{ij} : V_{ij} \xrightarrow{\sim} V_{ji}\), and triple-overlap
84	  data \(t'_{ijk}\) with its compatibility identity \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\). For an index
85	  triple \((i,j,k)\) write
86	  \[
87	    V_{ijk} \;=\; V_{ij} \times_{U_i} V_{ik}
88	  \]
89	  for the common triple overlap, with the two projections
90	  \(p^{ij}_{ijk} : V_{ijk} \to V_{ij}\) and \(p^{ik}_{ijk} : V_{ijk} \to V_{ik}\), and
91	  let \(t'_{ijk} : V_{ijk} \xrightarrow{\sim} V_{jki}\) be the glue datum's triple
92	  transition, satisfying
93	  \[
94	    t'_{ijk} \,\circ\, p^{jk}_{jki} \;=\; p^{ij}_{ijk} \,\circ\, t_{ij}
95	    \qquad (t_{\mathrm{fac}}).
96	  \]
97	  Given per-chart sheaves of modules \(\mathcal{M}_i\) and a transition isomorphism
98	  \[
99	    g_{ij} \;:\; f_{ij}^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i
100	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
101	    (t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_j
102	  \]
103	  on the overlap \(V_{ij}\) (as in \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}), the
104	  \emph{base-change transport} of \(g_{ij}\) to the triple overlap \(V_{ijk}\) is the
105	  isomorphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_{V_{ijk}}\)-modules
106	  \[
107	    \widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}
108	      \;:\;
109	    (f_{ij} \circ p^{ij}_{ijk})^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i
110	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
111	    (t_{ij} \circ f_{ji} \circ p^{ij}_{ijk})^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_j
112	  \]
113	  obtained by pulling \(g_{ij}\) back along \(p^{ij}_{ijk}\) and reassociating the
114	  iterated pullbacks via \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}. With the analogous transports
115	  \(\widehat{g}_{jk}^{\,i}\) and \(\widehat{g}_{ik}^{\,j}\) (the first taken along
116	  \(t'_{ijk}\), so that \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\) identifies its source and target sheaves
117	  with those of \(\widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}\) and \(\widehat{g}_{ik}^{\,j}\)), all three
118	  transported isomorphisms become endomorphism-composable: \(\widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}\)
119	  and \(\widehat{g}_{jk}^{\,i}\) land on a common sheaf, and their composite is
120	  comparable to \(\widehat{g}_{ik}^{\,j}\) on \(V_{ijk}\).
121	\end{lemma}
122	
123	\begin{proof}
124	  \uses{def:modules_pullbackComp, def:gr_the_glue_data}
125	  The pullback functor on sheaves of modules is a pseudofunctor: for composable
126	  scheme morphisms it carries the comparison isomorphisms of
127	  \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}, natural in the sheaf and coherent for triple
128	  composites. Pulling the transition isomorphism \(g_{ij}\) back along the projection
129	  \(p^{ij}_{ijk} : V_{ijk} \to V_{ij}\) yields an isomorphism between
130	  \((p^{ij}_{ijk})^{*} f_{ij}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i\) and \((p^{ij}_{ijk})^{*}(t_{ij} \circ
131	  f_{ji})^{*}\mathcal{M}_j\); applying \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp} to each side
132	  reassociates these into the single-pullback form
133	  \((f_{ij} \circ p^{ij}_{ijk})^{*}\mathcal{M}_i\) and
134	  \((t_{ij} \circ f_{ji} \circ p^{ij}_{ijk})^{*}\mathcal{M}_j\), giving
135	  \(\widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}\). For the \((j,k)\)-transition the same construction is
136	  applied along the triple transition \(t'_{ijk}\) rather than a bare projection; the
137	  glue datum's compatibility identity \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\),
138	  \(t'_{ijk} \circ p^{jk}_{jki} = p^{ij}_{ijk} \circ t_{ij}\), guarantees that the
139	  source of \(\widehat{g}_{jk}^{\,i}\) coincides with the target of
140	  \(\widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}\) (both are the pullback of \(\mathcal{M}_j\) along the same
141	  morphism \(V_{ijk} \to U_j\)), so the two are composable as endomorphism-style
142	  isomorphisms over \(V_{ijk}\). The reassociations introduced by
143	  \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp} are coherent (they satisfy the pentagon identity), so
144	  the composite \(\widehat{g}_{jk}^{\,i} \circ \widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}\) and the third
145	  transport \(\widehat{g}_{ik}^{\,j}\) are isomorphisms between the same pair of
146	  sheaves on \(V_{ijk}\), and may be compared directly. This is the module-level
147	  analogue of the scheme-level triangle \(t'_{ijk}\)/\(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\) and is the
148	  shape in which the multiplicative cocycle condition is stated below.
149	\end{proof}
150	
151	\begin{lemma}[Triple-overlap endpoint bridges]
152	  \label{lem:gr_glueData_bridges}
153	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_src,
154	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_mid,
155	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_tgt}
156	  \uses{def:gr_the_glue_data}
157	  Let \(D\) be a glue datum of schemes (\cref{def:gr_the_glue_data}) and fix
158	  indices \(i,j,k\) with triple overlap \(V_{ijk} = V_{ij} \times_{U_i} V_{ik}\).
159	  The three transports of \cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport} have
160	  endpoints pinned down by three scheme-morphism equalities on \(V_{ijk}\), each a
161	  pure consequence of the glue datum's structural identities:
162	  \begin{itemize}
163	    \item (\emph{src}) \(\mathrm{fst} \circ f_{ij} = \mathrm{snd} \circ f_{ik}\)
164	      -- the defining pullback condition of \(V_{ijk}\);
165	    \item (\emph{mid}) \(\mathrm{fst} \circ (t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})
166	      = (t'_{ijk} \circ \mathrm{fst}) \circ f_{jk}\) -- the pullback condition
167	      composed with the glue datum's \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\) identity;
168	    \item (\emph{tgt}) \((t'_{ijk} \circ \mathrm{fst}) \circ (t_{jk} \circ f_{kj})
169	      = \mathrm{snd} \circ (t_{ik} \circ f_{ki})\) -- the cocycle core, from the
170	      \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\) associativity, the reversed pullback condition, the
171	      \(t_{\mathrm{inv}}\) collapse and the glue datum's cocycle identity.
172	  \end{itemize}
173	  These three equalities align the source, middle, and target sheaves of the three
174	  transports so that the cocycle equation (C2) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}
175	  typechecks.
176	\end{lemma}
177	\begin{proof}
178	  \uses{def:gr_the_glue_data}
179	  Each equality is an identity of morphisms in the category of schemes, proved by
180	  rewriting with the glue datum's axioms: \emph{src} is literally
181	  \(\mathrm{pullback.condition}\); \emph{mid} adds the \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\)
182	  factorisation \(D.t_{\mathrm{fac}}\,i\,j\,k\); \emph{tgt} chains
183	  \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\)-associativity at \((j,k,i)\), the reversed pullback
184	  condition, an auxiliary collapse combining \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\)-associativity at
185	  \((k,i,j)\) with \(t_{\mathrm{inv}}\), and finally the cocycle identity
186	  \(D.\mathrm{cocycle}\,i\,j\,k\). No sheaf-of-modules data enters; these are
187	  bookkeeping equalities of the underlying scheme cover.
188	\end{proof}
